dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Those are the last 2 words I want to hear out of your mouth.

jokes.text: Good morning, master Sanders.
jokes.setup: Hank trying to act normal after being told not to show concern
jokes.punchline: Overcorrects with absurdly obsequious greeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - trying so hard to be normal he becomes bizarre

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Good morning, master Sanders.

jokes.text: I was a swimsuit model in the I can't believe it's a guy catalogue.
jokes.setup: Hank pitching a joke idea to Larry

jokes.explanation: Absurd concept that fits Hank's delusional self-image perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I was a swimsuit model in the I can't believe it's a guy catalogue.

jokes.text: What did I just tell you?

jokes.explanation: Simple but effective beat showing the immediate failure of Artie's plan

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: What did I just tell you?

jokes.text: I know, I know. I dodged a bullet.
jokes.setup: Hank's name wasn't mentioned in the brutal review of Larry
jokes.punchline: Hank's relief at not being criticized while his co-host is destroyed
jokes.explanation: Perfect selfish Hank moment - relieved about Larry's pain because he's unscathed

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I know, I know. I dodged a bullet.

jokes.text: And do what?
jokes.setup: Larry threatens to take a critic outside to fight
jokes.punchline: Beverly's skeptical question about what Larry would actually do
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Larry's tough guy posturing with two simple words

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: And do what?

jokes.text: hurt me as much as it hurt you. Really? Well, even though my... my name wasn't mentioned.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: hurt me as much as it hurt you.
Really?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Well, even though my... my name wasn't mentioned.

jokes.text: there is no new curtain.

jokes.explanation: Clever trap that exposes both Larry's paranoia and Hank's empty flattery

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: there is no new curtain.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What was wrong with last night's crowd? Where did they come in from? Whew!

jokes.text: He claims that he was just doing research for his starring role in his next big film, the Robert downey Jr. Story.
jokes.setup: Robert Downey Jr. in detox trying to shake drug problem

dialogue.line: He claims that he was just doing research for his starring role in his next big film, the Robert downey Jr. Story.

jokes.text: Actually, I believe it was the recycler. I believe that's what he writes for now.

jokes.explanation: Good defensive joke showing Larry's petty response to criticism

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Actually, I believe it was the recycler. I believe that's what he writes for now.

jokes.text: Tom, this is a needy talk show host, pal. This is a needy talk show host.
jokes.setup: Tom Shales called Larry needy in his review
jokes.punchline: Larry owns the criticism by stating it as obvious fact
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where Larry deflates criticism by embracing it completely

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Tom, this is a needy talk show host, pal. This is a needy talk show host.

jokes.text: I don't mind that he made a joke about me I just wish it was funny.

jokes.punchline: Shales' devastating comeback about the quality of Larry's joke
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - critiquing not just Larry's response but his comedic ability

dialogue.speaker: Tom Shales
dialogue.line: I don't mind that he made a joke about me I just wish it was funny.

jokes.text: The song? It's "king of pain." He's an asshole. He knows. He read it.

jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid belief that Sting chose the song to mock him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry paranoia - assuming everyone is commenting on his situation

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: The song? It's "king of pain." He's an asshole. He knows. He read it.

jokes.text: 'Cause it's Hank's.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: 'Cause it's Hank's.

dialogue.line: Dear dumbfuck

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Dear dumbfuck"?

dialogue.speaker: Larry

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, I thought it was about me, but maybe it's about you.

dialogue.line: Well, you, uh... you sit next to someone for 8 years, and you, uh... you think you know them, but, uh, you don't.

dialogue.line: I did that show. Bill maher hit on me

dialogue.speaker: Tom Shales

dialogue.speaker: Tom Shales

jokes.text: So, um... I forwarded your fax to that asshole this morning.

jokes.punchline: Reveals he sent Larry's angry fax to the critic anyway
jokes.explanation: Devastating revelation that perfectly captures Hank's toxic combination of sympathy and malice

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: So, um... I forwarded your fax to that asshole this morning.

jokes.text: You dumbfuck.
